title: Feeding difficulty
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Specifies weight and height measurement, clinical/developmental/social assessment, detailed feeding or eating history, observation, and assessment-directed investigations.
assessment.rationale: Supports clinical, developmental and social assessment plus feeding observation for child feeding difficulty; no decision is generated.

source.title: Faltering growth: recognition and management of faltering growth in children — Recommendations
source.population: Infants and children with weight loss or concerns about faltering growth.
source.setting: Primary, community, and specialist pediatric care.
source.exclusions: Exact for faltering-growth assessment and feeding history; partial for undifferentiated acute poor feeding and not directly validated for UAE practice.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Specifies weight and height measurement, clinical/developmental/social assessment, detailed feeding or eating history, observation, and assessment-directed investigations.

assessment.text: Specifies suckling, bottle feeding, routines, environment, aversion, interaction, appetite, food types, and feeding-diary domains.
assessment.rationale: Supports suckling, bottle feeding, routines, aversion, interaction, appetite and food types for child feeding difficulty; no decision is generated.

source.title: Faltering growth: recognition and management of faltering growth in children — Recommendations

assessment.text: Defines growth monitoring and referral for underlying disorder, failure to respond, slow growth, rapid weight loss, severe undernutrition, safeguarding concern, or acute illness.
assessment.rationale: Supports growth monitoring and clinician-led referral context for child feeding difficulty; no decision is generated.
